data.Modification: Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G

project.title: cryo-EM study of human liver mitochondria lysate
project.projectDescription: This is the proteomic analysis data of Human liver mitochondria lysate subjected to cryo-EM study.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Human liver mitochondria were lysed and fractionated by size exclusion chromatography. Three protein fractions were collected and subjected to protein digestion followed by LC-MS/MS analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Proteins were identified by comparing all of the experimental peptide MS/MS spectra against the UniProt human proteome database using a database search engine, MaxQuant (version 2.0.3.1). Carbamidomethylation of cysteine was set as a fixed modification, whereas variable modifications included oxidation of methionine to methionine sulfoxide and acetylation of N-terminal amino groups. For peptide/protein identification, strict trypsin specificity was applied, the minimum peptide length was set to 7, the maximum missed cleavage was set to 2, and the cutoff false discovery rate was set to 0.025.

labPIs.title: Dr
labPIs.affiliation: Department of Pharmacology, School of Medicine, Case Western Reserve University,cleveland,OH,USA
labPIs.name: Edward W

references.referenceLine: Zhang Z, Tringides ML, Morgan CE, Miyagi M, Mears JA, Hoppel CL, Yu EW. High-Resolution Structural Proteomics of Mitochondria Using the 'Build and Retrieve' Methodology. Mol Cell Proteomics. 2023 22(12):100666

Characteristics[MaterialType]: Mitochondria isolated from liver tissue were purchased from Xenotech (BioIVT).
Characteristics[ReductionReagent]: DTT was added to a final concentration of 10 mM at 25 °C for 30 min in order to reduce the sample.
Characteristics[AlkylationReagent]: Alkylation was achieved using 25 mM iodoacetamide at 25 °C for 30 min.
Characteristics[NumberOfSamples]: We isolated three peaks based on the column separation.
Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: one biological repeat for each peak
Comment[FractionationMethod]: enriched using a Superdex 200 column (GE Healthcare) equilibrated with 20 mM Tris–HCl (pH 7.5) and 100 mM NaCl.
Comment[Instrument]: LC-MS/MS was performed using a Thermo Scientific Fusion Lumos mass spectrometry system (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
Comment[GradientTime]: a linear gradient of acetonitrile (2–35%) in aqueous solution containing 0.1% formic acid was used for peptide chromatography for 90 min at a rate of 300 nl/min.
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: operating in data-dependent MS to MS/MS switching mode
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: The precursor ion tolerance was set to 4.5 ppm.
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: The product ion tolerance was 0.5 Da.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: the maximum missed cleavage was set to 2
